title: rubus × pascanensis
intro: Rubus × pascanensis, also known as the Himalayan blackberry or thimbleberry hybrid, is a plant species that belongs to the rose family and is native to the Himalayas. The Rubus × pascanensis plant has medicinal properties, with its leaves and stems containing anthocyanins, flavonoids, and other antioxidants that have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. This plant offers several health benefits, including reducing inflammation, improving cardiovascular health, and exhibiting antioxidant and anti-cancer properties. Rubus × pascanensis can be prepared as teas, infusions, or tinctures, and its berries are edible and can be used in jams, preserves, or eaten fresh.
